title: HEARING FROM GOD IN THREE EASY STEPS!
body: **1. Come into God’s presence in prayer, with quietness of body and spirit, and with a clean heart.**

We must come to God in quietness of mind and spirit. We will never hear from the Lord as long as we are in the midst of physical as well as mental noise and confusion. We have to stop everything else and get quiet.
Be open to anything God may want to show you. If your mind is made up about something, you need to let it go. We can’t be holding anything back from the Lord when we come to Him.
Nor can we be holding on to any unconfessed sin in our hearts. The Bible says, “If I regard iniquity in my heart the Lord will not hear me.” (Psalms 66:18)
For example, if you have something against someone in your household, maybe you should go to them and try to make things right between you. That sort of thing. At least give it a try.
So you have come to God in prayer with quietness of spirit and with a clean heart...
The next step is:
**2. You must have no will of your own and be open to whatever God has for you, whatever that may be.**
As long as we think we have all the answers God will not answer. God *cannot *answer.
He WILL guide us, if we seek His help. But we must seek it willingly and unconditionally. He never forces us to do anything.
So, it is when we have no will of our own and realize that we can’t do it, that God shows us.
Ok. You have come to God with quietness of spirit, you have no will of your own, and are open to whatever God has for you... now,
**3. Accept the first thing that comes to mind as from God.**
That first impression, thought, words, or idea is God. When you ask God for an answer, expect Him to answer and believe that the first thing that comes to mind is from Him.

As long as you are empty, as long as you know that you don’t have the answer and create an empty place in your heart God will fill it. He will show you the solution to your problem, the words to say, or the next step to take, whichever the case may be.

That first thing that comes to you when you cry out to Him is of the Lord and it will encourage your heart. It will guide you back to His will if you have gotten sidetracked, or it will keep you in His will if you are already there.
*Summary*
**1. Come to God in prayer with quietness of spirit and with a clean heart.**
**2. Have no will of your own. Be willing to accept His answer whatever it may be.**
**3. Accept the first thing that comes to mind as from God. That first impression, thought, words, or idea is from the Lord.**
THIS is how to receive from God.
Ask God to show you how these three points work. Ask Him to teach you to hear from Him.
These things at first may seem a little fuzzy, but as you try them God will show you how it works.
Just remember, God always speaks FIRST. Then the devil will try to come in and make you doubt what God has said.
Resist the temptation to over-think it, analyze it, question it, or delay in obeying it. Don’t listen to him! Don’t doubt what God has said.
As you put into practice these guidelines God will lead you, provide for you, and make you a blessing to many.
Our God is alive and He still speaks today. He gives the very best to those who seek Him and put Him first!
Hearing from God is EASY!
verse:
“What things ye desire when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.” (Mark 11:24)
“Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.” (Luke 11:9)
“Whatsoever ye ask in My name, that will I do.” (John 14:13)
“If ye abide in Me, and My words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.” (John 15:7)

title: BEFORE IT’S TOO LATE
body: Worldly voices are winning the young.
Today’s kids are being influenced and sucked
into all kinds of negative, self defeating habits and mindsets. Narrow minded, inflexible religious extremists turn young people away from God.
But young people today actually are not really against God. They are just against the false image of God created by these religious fanatics!
We speak to the young in spirit of all ages. We want to help them see that God is not a cruel tyrant and that not everything fun or pleasurable is sin.
God’s Truth is as good for us today as it has ever been. We must adapt if we are ever going to reach our younger generations.
Before it’s too late!
affirmation: I am flexible and change and adapt to meet the needs of today.
verse: “Behold I will do a new thing; now it shall spring forth; saith the Lord.” (Isaiah 43:19)

title: PRAYER LIKE PIZZA
body: People trust Pizza Hut more than God. People believe in Pizza Hut more than in God’s Word. How is that?
You make a phone call, place your order, hang up and wait for the doorbell to ring. You don’t keep calling and calling and calling them over and over and over again! How ridiculous would that be?

Yet that’s what people do to God. They pray for something, then they keep asking and asking, begging and pleading as if God were deaf. To make things worse they will often get everyone they know on board begging and pleading, often for days or weeks or years at a time!
All of their praying isn’t a sign of great faith. It is a sign of their great disbelief.
God isn’t deaf. He heard them the first time.
They aren’t waiting for God to answer: God is waiting for them to start trusting!
So, next time you pray, do as you do with pizza... Pray for it once. Then wait with quiet assurance until your doorbell rings!
affirmation: I know you hear me Lord. I know the answer is on its way from the very first second I pray!

title: WHILE YOU STILL HAVE TIME
subtitle: For Mother's Day
body: With my mother it was “love at first sight”. Haha! To a baby there is nothing better than being held in his mother’s arms.
I think a mother is the single greatest influence in a child’s life, second only to God.
Mothers are nice. Mothers represent everything soft and pure, good and noble. Mothers are there to heal your wounds, sew your clothes, and tuck you in at night.
No matter what you do, your mother will always love you. And you know that!
Mothers are the best cooks in the world. Mothers set the standards of what we seek in a wife, consciously or subconsciously. And a mother only wants the best for us... If we would but listen.
My mother died peacefully in her sleep not very long ago.
Enjoy yours while you still can. Show her that you love her!
affirmation: I love my mother and I show her that I do.
verse: “Her children arise up and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praises her.” (Proverbs 31:28)

title: HOLIDAY TWISTS
body: Over the years Easter has become less and less about Jesus, and more and more about a rabbit and eggs. How did that start?
And what do an egg and a rabbit even have in common?

Thanksgiving, the day the Pilgrims gave thanks to God for making it through their first winter in America having fled religious persecution in England, has now been reduced to gorging on turkey and watching football.

Then of course there is Christmas: Jesus, the manger, and the almighty God of all creation have been replaced with trees, snowmen, and a kindly old gentleman in a flying sleigh who brings everyone gifts.
These things started so long ago that we don’t even question them anymore.
What is happening here? Things have become so twisted.
No wonder people have stopped believing in Jesus, when He is placed on the same level as the Tooth Fairy!

title: GOOD READING
body: The book of Ecclesiastes has always been one of my favorite books of the Bible.
King Solomon wrote it and aside from being the richest man at the time he was also the wisest.
Ecclesiastes is a short book of only 12 chapters but there is some real meat packed in them.
I like to take my time and read it slowly and have enjoyed memorizing much of it over the years.
The book of Ecclesiastes has a lot of good and wise advice.
But most of all it helps me to keep the right perspective on what life is really about...
And why we are all here.

title: EASTER SUNDAY
body: Easter is the day that Jesus Christ rose from the dead. The day that our Lord conquered death, vanquished Satan, and gave us the power to do the same.
Our God is not dead, but alive, empowering us and blessing us as we yield to Him and let Him use us as instruments of His peace, of His mercy, and of His love.
Our God is a happy God who wants us to be happy too, and enjoy the wonderful life He’s given us, along with the pleasures, rewards, and fruits of our labor.
Our God is a God of love, who loves the unlovely with endless mercy and compassion, because He knows what it is like to be human, having walked this earth himself.
Our God is not dead but alive and living in the hearts of everyone that receives His free gift of salvation. Alive in the hearts of all those that love.
verse: “Why seek ye the living among the dead? He is not here but is risen. Then opened He their understanding, that they might understand the scriptures. And He said unto them, Go ye into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.” (Luke 24:5,6,45 & Mark 16:15)

title: THROW ‘EM OUT?
body: We drink a soft drink and throw away the can. We eat a candy bar and throw away the wrapper. We throw away an old toothbrush. We get rid of old shoes. We no longer want last year’s phone or our older model car.

Yesterday’s treasures have become today’s trash. Disposable silverware, plates, glasses and cups, disposable bottles, bags, boxes, and stuff that we no longer need or want or can use. Toilet paper and razors, motor oil and gloves... The list is endless of things we throw away.
People end a relationship over some little misunderstanding. They disown a friend over a small difference of opinion, or sometimes even for a few dollars! People are not supposed to be disposable.
Friends, friendships, people, and relationships are the most important things in God’s eyes.
The day will come when we will all meet again face to face in God’s presence. Including those we despised.

title: DUMB PEOPLE
body: Someone once said, “God must like dumb people because He made so many of them.”
The thing about dumb people is, they don’t even realize how dumb they are! And that’s what makes it so hard for the rest of us who have to put up with them.
God uses dumb people though. He uses them to teach us patience, tolerance, and love. Or so we hope.
I’m not sure what God teaches dumb people in this life. That’s always been a mystery to me.
And the thing that really bothers me is how HAPPY they can be.
Come to think of it...
Maybe they are not so dumb after all!
affirmation: I continually remind myself that I am not as smart as I think I am.
verse: “God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty.” (1 Corinthians 1:27)

title: TWO THINGS
body: A lot of people complain that they don’t have more money. They think they have it hard.
But if you have the Lord and do two things, God will not only bless you, but He will also cause you to grow and multiply. He will give you more!
FIRST: give a percentage every month of everything you make to someone else, to someone more needy, or to some good cause.
SECOND: start doing something concrete to share the Truth of God’s Word with those around you.
If you do these two things, God will never fail to bless and prosper you.
This simple rule remains the same: “Give, and ye shall receive.”
But you have to first start by doing the GIVING!
affirmation: I give of what I have both materially and spiritually, and God blesses me abundantly.
verse: “Every man shall give as he is able, according to the blessing of the Lord thy God which He hath given thee.” (Deuteronomy 16:17)

title: THAT PIECE OF PAPER
subtitle: (From I'm a Bad Christian)
body: Why are so many religious people obsessed with trying to control who has sex with who?
If you are not what they call *married*, which means having a piece of paper, they say you are “living in sin.” Yet so many people only have that piece of paper. That is the extent of their marriage!
According to most religions, you get that piece of paper and you are ok with God to have all the sex you want. Then, when you get bored, just get another piece of paper, and split up!

But then of course sex is out of the question they say, because if you don’t have that piece of paper you are *living in sin *again! To them, desire and sex are ok as long as you have a paper. But they become “lust and fornication” without that paper. How ridiculous.
God doesn’t care about a piece of paper. God looks at your heart. God’s only yardstick is love. All God sees are two people who love each other.
And two people who love each other, see a little bit of God!
verse: “God is love. He that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.” (1 John 4:16)

title: THE TIME IS NOW
body: Worldly preachers and New Age voices vie for our attention.
Too many of God’s own people seek treasure where there is only trash. They seek to quench their spiritual thirst with waters from worldly streams. Hence they never grow up spiritually.
Leadership training is long over. The school bell rang a long time ago.
God now wants each of His children to go straight to the source, receive their spiritual nourishment directly from Him, and share those things with this desperate, needy world.
“Feed His sheep” was Jesus’ commandment to His disciples then.
“FEED HIS SHEEP” is His commandment to us TODAY.
affirmation: As I give out God’s life giving words to others, I too am fed and blessed by Him.
verse: “I have not hid Thy righteousness within my heart; I have declared Thy faithfulness and Thy salvation.” (Psalms 40:10)

title: ISN’T IT GREAT!
body: No one makes coffee like my daughter.
My daughter hits the nail right on the head with the most perfect combination of coffee, milk and sugar. And she serves it to me at just the right temperature. A real treat.
And I can’t seem to ever get it wrong when I make coffee for her, although every single time I make it different!
I play it by ear and vary something every time so as to create some variety, so I say. (Or might it be out of laziness?) No matter what I do, I just never get it wrong.
She always says I make the yummiest coffee!
So what’s the lesson?
I don’t think there is a lesson here.
But isn’t it GREAT to feel LOVED!?
affirmation: Thank You God for Your love in my life and in my heart.
verse:
“Ah, sweet mystery of life, at last I have found thee.
At last I have found the reason for it all.
‘Tis love, and love alone, the whole world yearns for.
And ‘tis love that bids them to heed Thy call.” (Robert Burns)

title: MISFITS
body: Many people today feel like outcasts. They
are misfits.

They have never really fit into the System’s squeaky-clean image of what a believer in Christ is *supposed *to be. They love the Lord, enjoy a cold one while watching the game, think fishing is fun, dancing is cool and don’t believe everything pleasurable is a sin. They like most things everyone else does, but they still somehow never quite fit into the religious system.
That’s because God is calling them out.
God is calling out a people unto Himself.
People who do not fit in. People who are looking

up and seeing something more. People who are seeing something greater. People who are catching a glimpse of God’s loving plan of universal restitution and eternal happiness for all of mankind... God’s happy ending. That’s what’s happening.
Do you feel like a misfit?
Welcome to God’s Kingdom!

title: CLOSE TO HIM!
body: The planet is warming. The ocean is rising.
The ozone is depleting. The coral disappearing.
Earthquakes are increasing. The forests depleting.
Crime is increasing.
Fear is increasing.
Greed is increasing.
Love is decreasing.
God’s grace is increasing.
Stay close to HIM!
affirmation: All I ever wanted was You, dear Jesus.
verse: “My soul trusteth in Thee, O God. Under Thy wings I will make my refuge until these calamities be past.” (Psalms 57:1)

title: START TODAY
body: The winds of change are blowing upon our planet. We’re living in a world different from the one our parents knew. There is an ideological war being waged, not only for the hearts and minds of men but also for their usefulness for God during this life.

But the majority of people have been lulled to sleep by the cares and worries of their everyday lives. They spend their time in frivolous pursuits and don’t see the storm clouds forming over the horizon. The day will come when we will not enjoy the freedoms we now have.

It’s time to get down to business for the Lord. It’s time to stop living selfishly and start thinking of others. You will receive more only as you start learning to give. You will receive the spiritual feeding you seek only as you start giving out to others.

One way you can do this is by reading these Reflections with your family and praying together before starting your day, and then getting online and sharing them with your contacts and friends and encouraging them to do the same.
START TODAY, and God will start moving more in your life.
What are you waiting for?

title: PARTING WORDS…
body: **No one could more beautifully or eloquently sum up life, love, and what our sojourn here on this earth is all about than Betty J. Eadie. We close with the following passage from one of her wonderful books:**
“As I stood in the presence of Christ he spoke these words: ‘Above all else, love one another.’ He was firm, emphatic.
This was the most important truth I would learn in my experience with Him, and He wanted me to understand it, to feel its full impact. It is the most important thing we can know.

To love is the reason we exist. To learn to love more fully is the reason we have come to earth. Love is the energy which holds the universe in place. It is the gateway through which we return to our Creator.
Love can conquer any problem, ease any suffering, heal any disease. Love creates and magnifies the joys of life. And at the moment of death, love turns all earthly pain into indescribable bliss.
We were conceived in Love spiritually, and Love is the center of our beings. It is the energy of our souls, the spark of our divine nature.
Being made of love, we cleave to it and seek it in all that we do. When we do not have it, or when we have lost it, we grieve.
Its presence or absence colors our every action. It is life. It is happiness. It is salvation itself. ‘God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.’ (1 John 4:16)
On earth our spirits miss the electrifying power of God’s love. Our minds are veiled, but our spirits remember His love, and silently we cry for its comfort and soothing warmth.
Some of us spend our lives weaving in and out of relationships, searching for the essence  of this divine emotion, hunting it, craving it, but never finding it in this world.
Some of us cry to God for this love, expecting Him to send it to us through another person.
But it first must come from ourselves.”
(Betty J. Eadie “The Ripple Effect”)
